rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TUAN VAN TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: Upon review of the recently verified supplement originally filed on 9/13/23, it appears that the issues raised in Examiner's Notes, which were sent on 4/27/23, have still not fully been responded to. The remaining issues are as follows: a. Petitioners allege that bond of $1,000,000 is sufficient, but no calculation is provided. It is noted that the FMV of all assets of the estate on hand as of the end of the account (1/3/23) is alleged to be $1,433,175. An ex parte petition to increase bond will need to be filed if bond needs to be increased. b. Petitioners have not alleged length of time needed to close the estate. c. Notice of Hearing on the mandatory Judicial Council Form has not been sent. NOTE: the amended Notice of Hearing filed on 5/3/23 provided the incorrect hearing date and only provided notice to counsel for the GAL (direct notice to the GAL is also required). d. Proposed order has not been submitted, which should also be e-lodged (via e-filing). e. The declaration pursuant to Local Rule 14.59A (certifying that the original document(s) will be available for inspection in the same way as documents are maintained under CRC 2.257) has not been filed for the bank statement attached to the 9/13/23 supplement. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD EDWARD HERINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of service filed on 4/3/24 is incomplete at item 2. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Real property taxes have been paid and fiduciary income tax returns have been prepared and filed..? Paragraphs 20 and 21 of the report do not appear to be accurate. Deadline is 6/7/24 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The wife and the daughter are the only heirs, and the only party entitled to notice would be the wife. The administrator/petitioner does not need to give notice to herself. [Probate Code Section 1201.] When a spouse and issue survived, more remote heirs are not interested parties unless they are creditors.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHNNY J. AND MARGIE CHERRY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions Evidence Sanctions Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [part 1 of 2 - Cherry Trust] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Now before the Court is Petitioner Windy Cherry's Motion for an Order Imposing (1) Terminating Sanctions; (2) Evidence Sanctions; (3) Issue Sanctions; and (4) Monetary Sanctions. By way of background, on January 24, 2024, this court entered the following discovery orders: (1) Order Granting Motion For An Order Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories Set 1 And Set 2 And Imposing Monetary Sanctions; (2) Order Granting Motion For An Order Compelling Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Set 1 And Imposing Monetary Sanctions; (3) Order Granting Motion For An Order Deeming Matters Specified In Requests For Admission Set 1 And Set 2 Admitted And Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Petitioner filed the instant motion on February 28, 2024 after Respondents failed to comply with the January 24, 2024 discovery orders, and a hearing was set for March 27, 2024. Respondents filed a late Opposition to the Motion. A Reply to the Opposition was filed by Petitioner, along with an Objection and Request to Strike the Declaration of Clinton Killian filed in support of the late Opposition. On March 22, 2024, the Court continued the hearing on Petitioner's motion for sanctions and issued an Order to Show Cause ordering Respondents and their attorneys, Clinton Killian and Dorianne Romero Plihon, to appear and show cause on May 15, 2024, why their Response in Opposition to the First Amended Petition filed April 28, 2023 should not be stricken as a terminating sanction for their misuse of discovery in failing to comply with the January 24, 2024 Orders of this Court. Attorney Clinton Killian filed a verified response to the OSC along with a declaration from Nadar Fannyan, Mr. Killian's former law partner. Petitioner's attorney, Ray Robinson, also filed a responsive declaration to the OSC. In their response to the OSC, Respondents attached as Exhibit C evidence purporting to show that Respondents had complied with the discovery orders and responded to Petitioner's discovery. Exhibit C is comprised of: (i) Respondents' responses to Request for Admissions Set 1 and Set 2; (ii) Delphine Cherry's verified responses to Request for Production of Documents Set 1; (iii) Jimmy Cherry's unverified responses to Request for Production of Documents Set 1; (iv) Respondents' unverified responses to Special Interrogatories Set 1 and Set 2, made in their official capacity. Based on the record before the Court, Respondents have not fully responded to Petitioner's discovery and continue to be in violation of the Janaury 24, 2024 discovery orders. To wit, there is no showing that verified responses to special interrogatories, sets 1 or 2 were served, in either Respondents' individual capacity or in their official capacity; no documents have been produced; and the Court-imposed sanctions totaling $5,242.50 have not been paid. Moreover, the unverified responses to Special Interrogatories Set 1 and Set 2 made in Respondents' official capacity are deficient. Many of the responses refer to previous answers in violation of C.C.P. sec 2030.220(a). Based on the foregoing, Respondent's request for monetary sanctions, evidence and issue sanctions, and terminating sanctions is GRANTED, in part, pending a further hearing on June 12, 2024 at 2:30 p.m. (END OF PART 1 OF 2).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JOHNNY J. AND MARGIE CHERRY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions Evidence Sanctions Issue Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [PART 2 OF 2] Monetary sanctions are awarded under CCP sections 2031.310(h) and 2031.310(i), based on both Respondents' failure to obey the Court's January 24, 2024 orders, and unsuccessful opposition to the instant motion, without a showing that such an imposition would be unjust. Respondents Jimmy Cherry and Delphine Cherry and the law firm of Clinton Killian, Attorney at Law, APC are jointly and severally ordered to pay Petitioner $2,497.50 in monetary sanctions within 5 calendar days of the May 15, 2024 hearing. (Sanctions award amount calculated based on Decl. of Robinson ISO Motion at par. 9.) Petitioner's request for terminating sanctions is hereby continued to June 12, 2024 at 2:30 p.m. to give Respondents one final opportunity to comply with this Court's directives and properly respond to discovery. To comply with this Court's directives, Respondents must: " Pay the previously sanctioned amount of $5,242.50 to Petitioner by May 20, 2024. " Pay the new sanctions in the amount of $2,497.50 to Petitioner by May 20, 2024. " Serve Delphine Cherry's responses to Special Interrogatories Set 1 and Set 2, without objections, in her individual and in her official capacity. These responses must be verified and code compliant and served no later than June 4, 2024. " Serve Jimmy Cherry's responses to Special Interrogatories Set 1 and Set 2, without objections, in his individual and in his official capacity. These responses must be verified and code compliant and served no later than June 4, 2024. " Produce all documents in response to Petitioner's Request for Production of Documents Set 1. Documents shall be produced no later than June 4, 2024. " Re-file the Declaration of Clinton Killian, filed March 20, 2024, complying with C.C.P. sec 2015.5, no later than June 4, 2024. Failure to materially comply with any of the Court's directives will result in terminating sanctions at the hearing on June 12, 2024. Petitioner's counsel shall file a declaration no later than June 7, 2024 informing the Court whether Respondents have materially complied with the Court's directives above. If Respondents materially comply, the June 12, 2024 hearing will go off calendar. If not, the Court will hold the hearing with the intent to grant Petitioner's request for terminating sanctions. Petitioner Windy Cherry shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. (END OF TENTATIVE RULING).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE DAVID LIVING TRUST, DATED 7/7/22
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A second amended petition was filed on 4/15/24. No courtesy copy was received by, or even after, the 5/6/24 deadline, so it has not yet been reviewed. (2) Notice was not given to Carl Feinberg, Richard Feinberg, Henry Feinberg, and Maria Danziger at least 30 days before this hearing. (3) No proof of personal service of notice to Jeffrey M. Feinberg has been filed yet. (4) An unfiled copy of the attorney's declaration shows that heirs are incomplete. If the paternal (not maternal) aunt Ida Feinberg had four children who are now deceased, who are those children's issue (who would be Decedent's first cousins once, or more remotely, removed)? Were maternal first cousins Linda A. Blub Danziger and John R. Danziger not survived by issue, who would also be Decedent's first cousins once removed, or more remote? (The declaration also does not indicate dates of death for the uncles and aunts.) (5) None of the proofs of personal service has a copy of the Notice of Hearing attached to it with the printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance. Deadline is 6/7/24 to deliver to the assigned examiner courtesy copies of the second amended petition and any other papers filed to address these issues. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIA CONEY CORKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/24. (No earlier availability and incomplete, late response to issues posted for the 4/3/24 hearing.) Additional issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner yet. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The only correct form of notice at this initial stage of Probate is the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate, not a Notice of Hearing that doesn't even show the title of the pleading to be heard. (3) The correct Notice of Petition to Administer Estate must be mailed to all interested parties, showing the 6/18/24 hearing date and having a copy of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall attached to it. (4) Please file proof of the petitioner's appointment as the administrator of Percy Corke's estate. (5) Bond is required for a non-resident personal representative, notwithstanding the unknown value of the estate. Waivers of bond have not been filed. (6) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Deadline is 6/7/24 for the proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to be received by the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: Before final distribution can be ordered, Letters will need to be filed, certified no more than 30 days before the hearing of that petition for final distribution, proving that Sheryl Corke is then still the administrator of Percy Corke's estate. [LRSF 14.35 G.])

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/18/24. (No earlier availability and incomplete/late responses to issues posted for the 4/3/24 hearing.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have ATTACHED to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall. (3) A proposed personal representative must sign and verify his/her/their own pleadings, including any supplements or amendments thereto. [CRC 7.103(c).] New deadline is 6/7/24. A proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these (and prior) issues must be received by the assigned examiner by that date in order to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIU CHUEN DAVID TAM SEPARATE PROPERTY TRUST DATED JANUARY 6, 2017 FOR THE BENEFIT OF TAM FAMILY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitin For Order Approving Modification Of Trust Terms Under Probate Code Section 15403
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/2/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Trust petitions require 30 days' notice. (4) Ewa Tam was named as the successor trustee after Ming Joseph Chai. Please file a copy of his resignation and the consents or appointments evidencing Petitioner's standing as the successor trustee. (5) The proposed "restatement" will not be approved. There are provisions that do not make sense (Santa Clara case?) or go far beyond what the operative trust provides (distributions to the spouse?). If the only reason for this modification is because Estafania does not wish to receive any distribution, only Section 2.04 would need to be modified. (6) How old is the minor? He is entitled to separate and direct notice if he is at 12 years old or older. [CRC 7.51(d).] It appears that an amendment to this petition or a fully amended petition should be filed. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/18/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RONALD AND BETTY HO LIVING TRUST DATED 8/19/98
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Co-Trustee, Establishing Trustee Succession, Ascertaining Remainder Trust Beneficiaries, And Confirming Handwritten
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
